content.problemStatement:
There are NNN breakfasts in the restaurant `‚ÄúFat Hut‚Äù` where the Chef works. The ithi^{th}ith breakfast has an attractiveness AiA_iAi‚Äã and cost CiC_iCi‚Äã.

The Chef has noticed that nobody takes the jthj^{th}jth breakfast if there exists at least one breakfast iii such that Ai‚â•AjA_i \ge A_jAi‚Äã‚â•Aj‚Äã and Ci<CjC_i \lt C_jCi‚Äã<Cj‚Äã.

In other words, if a breakfast is less attractive and more expensive than any of the other dishes, then nobody is interested in that breakfast.

The Chef will be happy if all the NNN breakfasts have a chance to be taken. Unfortunately, the Chef has no power over prices. On the other hand, he can change the attractiveness of some breakfasts by some real number. However, after the changes, the attractiveness of the ithi^{th}ith breakfast must lie in the interval [Li,Ri][L_i,R_i][Li‚Äã,Ri‚Äã].

He would also like to change the attractiveness of the minimum number of breakfasts. Help the Chef do it.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each testcase contains N+1N + 1N+1 lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - number of breakfasts.
NNN  lines follow. For each valid iii, the ithi^{th}ith of these lines contains four space-separated integers AiA_iAi‚Äã, CiC_iCi‚Äã, LiL_iLi‚Äã, RiR_iRi‚Äã - current attractiveness, cost, the minimal and maximal allowed attractiveness after change of iii-th breakfast.


Output Format
For each test case, print in a single line containing one integer - minimum number of breakfasts the Chef should change so that all the NNN breakfasts have a chance to be taken. Print `"-1"`(without quotes) if it is impossible to achieve the goal.
constraints: 1‚â§Li‚â§Ai‚â§Ri‚â§1091 \le L_i \le A_i \le R_i \le 10^91‚â§Li‚Äã‚â§Ai‚Äã‚â§Ri‚Äã‚â§109 for each valid iii
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
The initial AiA_iAi‚Äã-s are pairwise distinct
The initial CiC_iCi‚Äã-s are pairwise distinct

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Chef can change first breakfast's attractiveness to 111. After that, all the 333 breakfasts have a chance to be taken. So the answer is 111.
Test case 222: Chef can change first breakfast's attractiveness to 222 and third breakfast's attractiveness to 2.52.52.5. There is no possible way to change the attractiveness of only one breakfast so that the condition is fulfilled. So the answer is 222.

Explanation:
Test case 111: Everything is fine already, so Chef has no need to change anything, and the answer is 000.
